name: Sierra de Albarracín - Tremedal Hermitage Loop
name_original: Ermita del Tremedal - circular norte Sierra de Albarracín
description: This difficult 38.9-mile road cycling route in Sierra de Albarracín offers challenging climbs, dense pine forests, and panoramic views from
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bronchales are situated in the Sierra de Albarracín, offering high-altitude terrain for cyclists. The region features dense Scots pine forests and karstic landscapes, with elevations reaching up to 1,854 meters at the Sierra Alta summit. Well-paved surfaces characterize many routes, providing challenging climbs and descents through varied natural features. Best road cycling routes around Bronchales The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Albarracín - Tremedal Hermitage Loop, a 38.9 miles (62.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Griegos – Puerto de Noguera (1695 m) loop from Bronchales, a difficult 51.4 miles (82.7 km) path. This route includes a climb to Puerto de Noguera at 1,695 meters, offering panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Bronchales Town Center – Orihuela del Tremedal loop from Noguera de Albarracín, a 47.8 miles (76.9 km) trail leading through high-altitude pine forests, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Bronchales is defined by high-altitude terrain, dense pine forests, and challenging climbs, with routes connecting scenic villages. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, catering to varied cycling preferences. The routes in Bronchales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bronchales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bronchales?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Bronchales, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bronchales?
A: Road cycling around Bronchales is characterized by high-altitude terrain, dense Scots pine forests, and karstic landscapes. You can expect challenging climbs and thrilling descents, with elevations reaching up to 1,854 meters at the Sierra Alta summit. Many routes feature well-paved surfaces, ensuring a smooth ride through varied natural features.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the routes around Bronchales cater to various skill levels. While many are classified as difficult, offering significant elevation gains and challenging ascents, there are also moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín – Guadalaviar Gorge loop from Noguera de Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal – Bronchales Town Center loop from Bronchales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter extensive Scots pine forests, numerous natural springs like Fuente del Canto, and interesting karstic landscapes. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3620455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of the Enchanted Forest of Brigitta&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by charming villages like Griegos and Orihuela del Tremedal.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Bronchales?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Bronchales are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963678309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Albarracín - Tremedal Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos – Puerto de Noguera (1695 m) loop from Bronchales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Bronchales?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 350 road cyclists. Reviewers often praise the challenging high-altitude terrain, the stunning dense pine forests, and the well-paved surfaces that make for an engaging ride. The opportunity for altitude training and the scenic villages connected by the routes are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is altitude a factor when road cycling in Bronchales?
A: Absolutely. Bronchales is situated at an elevation of 1,569 meters, with nearby Griegos at 1,601 meters, and the Sierra Alta summit reaching approximately 1,854 meters. This high altitude provides excellent opportunities for altitude training and means you should be prepared for significant climbs and descents, which can be more demanding than at lower elevations.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or rest along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its abundant natural springs and fountains, such as Fombuena, Fuente del Hierro, and Fuente del Pilar, which offer refreshing stops. Cycling routes also often connect charming villages like Griegos and Orihuela del Tremedal, where you might find local cafes or services.

Q: Can I find guided tours or e-bike rentals in Bronchales?
A: Yes, local services like Bronchales Experience offer guided cycling tours, including e-bike rentals. This makes the challenging terrain accessible to a wider range of fitness levels and allows individuals to customize their routes and enjoy the scenery at their own pace.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Bronchales?
A: Given its high altitude, the warmer months, typically late spring through early autumn, are generally the most favorable for road cycling in Bronchales. During these periods, the weather is more stable, and the temperatures are pleasant for tackling the climbs and descents. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Bronchales?
A: Yes, Bronchales is a stopping point on the historic Camino del Cid, with specific stages suitable for cycling. This indicates established and recognized routes that can be combined for longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos – Puerto de Noguera (1695 m) loop from Bronchales&lt;/a&gt;, at over 82 km, is an example of a challenging long-distance option.

Q: Are the road surfaces generally well-maintained for road cycling?
A: Many road cycling routes in the Bronchales area feature well-paved surfaces, ensuring a smooth ride. However, as with any mountainous region, some segments might be unpaved or more challenging, so it's always good to check route details before you go.
tips: The Puerto de Noguera is an ascent that leads from Noguera d'Albarracín in the direction of the center of the Serra of the same name. Its maximum altitude is 1,695 meters and it is surrounded by typical pine trees of the area.
Orihuela del Tremedal has its roots in medieval times. It is believed that the town was founded in the 12th century, during the Reconquista, when Christians recovered territories from the Muslims.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While the main ascent to the Ermita del Tremedal is manageable, it features initial steep sections with grades of 10-11% out of Orihuela del Tremedal, requiring a good level of fitness. The overall distance and elevation gain contribute to its challenging nature.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route, meaning you will end your ride at or near your starting point without having to retrace your path.

faqs.question: Where is the best starting point for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: A common and recommended starting point for the ascent to the Ermita del Tremedal is the charming village of Orihuela del Tremedal. This village is rooted in medieval times and offers a good base for your ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within or near the village of Orihuela del Tremedal, which serves as a primary starting point for the climb to the hermitage.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads suitable for road cycling. You'll experience dense pine forests, mainly black and Scots pine, providing a refreshing backdrop. Upon reaching the Tremedal Hermitage, you'll be rewarded with expansive views of the valley and the surrounding Sierra de Albarracín from a 'natural balcony'. The area also features unique geological formations like 'rivers of stone' and granite near the sanctuary.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the challenging yet rewarding climb to the Tremedal Hermitage, offering panoramic views. You'll also pass through the picturesque Orihuela del Tremedal and traverse the Puerto de Noguera, a notable mountain pass.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Albarracín region is generally best enjoyed during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions for road cycling, avoiding the colder temperatures and potential snow of winter, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Sierra de Albarracín?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general area of the Sierra de Albarracín. It is freely accessible for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Albarracín is generally welcoming to dogs in natural areas, this specific route is designed for road cycling. Bringing a dog along on a road bike can be challenging and is generally not recommended for their safety or yours on a route of this difficulty.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. Notable sections include parts of Las Tres Taifas (30%), MontañasVacías (20%), and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT (16%). You'll also find smaller segments of the Camino del Cid (Senderista) and [CIMA TE04] Nuestra Señora de Tremedal.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the route to be particularly cautious about?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the main ascent road is generally suitable for road cycling, it's important to note that a return via the 'Camino Viejo de la Ermita' is specifically advised against for inexperienced cyclists due to potentially dangerous and difficult sections. Stick to the main paved roads for a safer experience.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: The village of Orihuela del Tremedal, a key point on the route, is a charming settlement that likely offers basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options for cyclists looking to refuel or stay overnight.
